summary: Joel recommends the new edition of Danny Goodman's Dynamic HTML reference book. He praises the first edition as the best HTML reference despite being outdated, and notes the new 1400-page edition covers what browsers actually do in practice. He considers it essential for anyone working with HTML.
key_points: Danny Goodman's Dynamic HTML reference has been updated after several years
The book's value lies in documenting what browsers actually do, not just what specs say
At 1400 pages, it serves as a comprehensive practical reference for web development
money_quotes: It's been a few years since the first edition, which is still the best reference on HTML despite being severely out of date.
1400 packed pages that actually tells you what web browsers that are actually in use actually do, which makes it invaluable.
key_insight: The most valuable technical references document how things actually work in practice, not just what the specifications say.
